data.title: MainStreet Bancshares announces CFO retirement
data.description:
MainStreet Bancshares (MNSB) on Wednesday said that CFO Thomas Chmelik will retire from the post on December 31, 2025.

Chmelik is set to continue to serve as senior executive vice president and secretary of MainStreet Bank and the financial holding company through December 31, 2027.

data.title: Correlation no more: Nasdaq’s comeback leaves bitcoin in the dust
data.description:
Apollo’s chief economist, Torsten Slok, is drawing attention to a growing divergence between bitcoin (BTC-USD) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), two assets that have historically moved in close alignment.

While both are typically regarded as risk-on plays and often respond similarly to shifts in investor sentiment, that relationship has fractured in recent weeks, Slok noted.

The Nasdaq Composite has been steadily clawing back ground, narrowing its distance from record levels following the earlier month’s market turbulence. The tech-heavy benchmark now sits roughly 4% below its all-time high, reflecting renewed confidence in the sector and a broader appetite for growth-oriented equities. 

Bitcoin, however, has taken a markedly different path. The cryptocurrency has suffered a much steeper pullback, sliding more than 31% from its peak as traders reassess digital-asset valuations amid heightened volatility.

The widening gap between equity markets and bitcoin performance has raised questions about whether crypto is breaking away from traditional risk-asset behavior or simply undergoing a deeper cyclical correction.

Slok’s chart, referenced below, illustrates the notable breakdown in the once-strong correlation—an emerging trend that analysts are watching closely as markets attempt to recalibrate heading into year-end.

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QQQM), (SQQQ), (TQQQ), (QLD), and  (QID).

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).

  • Thanksgiving leftovers no one wants: 10 worst S&P 500 stocks of 2025
  • Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and 10 retail stocks to watch
  • Don't Buy This Dip: It's Black Friday In The Equity Market

data.title: Weekly ETF flows: Eight out of 11 sectors record outflows; Technology sector leads with higher inflows
data.description:
The world's largest exchange-traded fund, SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY), saw an inflow of $7.57 billion for the week ending November 21. However, the broader index tracking SPDR S&P 500 Trust (SPY) declined ~1% during the last week.

iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) saw an outflow of $1.09 billion last week. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) also slipped nearly 9% last week. Gold SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD) also recorded an outflow totaling $82.63 million last week. However, GLD gained 0.70% last week.

Last week’s inflows/outflows

The 11 S&P 500 sector tracking ETFs, together, recorded a net outflow of about $1.75 billion last week, according to the data from etfdb.com.

Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) led the outflow as eight out of 11 sectors saw money flowing out of their respective sector wise funds. The Financial Sector (XLY) saw an outflow of $1.02 billion, followed by Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) with $642.84 million flowing out last week. The Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) recorded an outflow of $621.21 million last week.

The highest inflow of funds last week was seen in the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK), with an inflow totaling $773.51 million, followed by the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV), which saw an inflow totaling $233.27 million. The Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI) recorded an inflow of $65.06 million last week.

Breakdown of S&P 500 sector fund flows:

Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF): ($1.02B)

Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY): ($642.84M)

Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE): ($621.21M)

Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE): ($30.35M)

Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI): $65.06M

Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV): $233.27M

Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK): $773.51M

Commodities and Bitcoin ETF fund flows:

Gold SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD): ($82.63M)

Silver iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV): $464.52M

iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT): ($1.09B)

ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI): $16.16M

data.title: Lennar applies 8.6% pro ration factor to Millrose exchange offer
data.description:
In its move to divest its remaining 20% stake in Millrose Properties (MRP), Lennar (LEN)  will apply an 8.604228% pro ration factor to 84.5M shares of Lennar shares tendered in an exchange offer, the company said on Wednesday.

Homebuilder Lennar (LEN) offered to exchange up to 33.3M shares of Millrose (MRP) class A stock it owned for outstanding shares of Lennar class A common stock. The offer expired at 12:00 AM, New York time, on Nov. 21, 2025. 

Because the number of shares tendered exceeded the offer's maximum number, Lennar (LEN) accepted only a portion of the shares of stock that were validly tendered on a pro rata basis. Stockholders who owned fewer than 100 shares of Lennar class A common stock, what's called an "odd-lot," and tendered their shares weren't subject to the proration. 

Lennar (LEN) accepted 8.05M shares of Lennar class A common stock in exchange for 33.3M shares of Millfose (MRP) class A common stock. In the final count, a total of ~85.3M shares were tendered, including the odd-lot holdings that aren't subject to proration.

Lennar (LEN) stock rose 0.3% and Millrose Properties (MRP) stock fell 0.7% in Wednesday premarket trading.

data.title: Nutanix slumps after Q1 revenue, outlook miss as analysts mull shift in revenue timing
data.description:
Shares of Nutanix (NTNX) declined about 12% premarket on Wednesday after first quarter revenue and second outlook missed estimates, drawing mixed reactions from analysts.

Needham kept its Buy rating on the shares of the enterprise cloud platform provider but lowered the price target to $65 from $80.

"Late in 1QFY26, Nutanix saw a greater mix of orders with future start-dates which does not impact Free Cash Flow, but detracts from in-quarter Revenue. If business with future start dates aligned with assumptions, Nutanix would have outperformed the high-end of Revenue (indicating more than a $9.4 Million (1.4%) impact compared to the reported $670.6 Million in Total Revenue). We find the $80 Million (2.7%) cut to FY26 Revenue guidance puzzling, given the size of the reduction which implies a significant chunk of business is not expected to ship until FY27," said analysts led by Mike Cikos.

In addition, the analysts said that the volume of business with future start dates is surprising given VMware displacements have been underway for multiple quarters. However, the analysts see these dynamics as only a shift in revenue timing, and remain positive as they believe the revenue cut will prove conservative.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Overweight rating on Nutanix's shares but cut the price target to $82 from $90.

"A Q1 rev [revenue] miss and lowered FY26 top-line outlook are messy but largely reflect rev rec [record] timing on land-expand deals. With NNARR [Net New Annual Recurring Revenue] accelerating, our thesis remains intact, but we lower PT to $82 from $90 on lower revenue visibility," said analysts led by Sanjit Singh.

The analysts noted that first quarter bookings came in above management's expectations but towards the end of the quarter there was a higher mix of land and expand deals that had future start dates resulting in revenue pushing out of the quarter.

If the mix of such deals had come in line with management's initial expectations, first quarter revenue would have come in above the high end of guidance (implying around $10M impact), the analysts added.

"One clear takeaway is that management does not expect this dynamic to be temporary as this higher mix of delay with deferred start dates reflects two factors. First, from a customer behavior standpoint, customers are looking for increased flexibility on contract start dates to align with their VMware migration plans. Second, Nutanix is seeing a higher mix of revenue coming from OEM [Original Equipment Manufacturer] partners (such as Dell, Cisco, Lenovo) and revenue can only be recognized once the OEM ships the hardware appliance – a dynamic that is not under Nutanix's control," said Singh and his team.

Related stocks: NetApp (NTAP) jumped about 6% premarket after its second quarter results, while Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) rose around 1%.

data.title: CTS appoints Pratik Trivedi COO
data.description:
CTS (CTS) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Pratik Trivedi as COO, effective December 5.

Trivedi is the senior vice president at the sensors, actuators, and electronic components manufacturing company.

data.title: DeFi Development reports insider purchases
data.description:
DeFi Development (DFDV) announced on Wednesday that its COO Parker White acquired 10,044 shares for $69,420 on November 25 in open market transactions.

Chief Strategy Officer Daniel Kang purchased 4,200 shares for $28,980.

Shares were +2.71% pre-market to $7.19.

data.title: FibroBiologics announces payoff of outstanding debt
data.description:
• FibroBiologics (FBLG) said on Wednesday that it has paid all amounts outstanding under the convertible promissory notes issued to YA II PN, an investment fund managed by Yorkville Advisors pursuant to the Standby Equity Purchase Agreement entered into on December 20, 2024, between the company and Yorkville.
  • The SEPA allows the company to sell up to $25M in the aggregate of its common stock to Yorkville over the course of two years. 
  • Yorkville advanced to FibroBiologics the first $15M available under the SEPA in three equal tranches of notes. 
  • These notes are no longer outstanding, and there will be no further conversions into shares of the company's common stock. 
  • The SEPA remains in place until December 20, 2026, allowing FibroBiologics, at its discretion, to sell an additional $10M of its common stock to Yorkville, subject to certain conditions.
  • This financial milestone comes as FibroBiologics advances toward key clinical and regulatory milestones, including the planned initiation of its Phase 1/2 clinical trial evaluating CYWC628, its fibroblast-based spheroid product candidate, in diabetic foot ulcer patients in early 2026. 
  • The company is also preparing to submit Investigational New Drug applications for the treatment of psoriasis with CYPS317 and the treatment of multiple sclerosis with CYMS101. 
  • FibroBiologics (FBLG) shares were down more than 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Amaze shares jump as it discloses insider share purchase
data.description:
• Amaze Holdings (AMZE) said on Wednesday that CEO Aaron Day purchased 335,440 shares of the company's stock in the open market following the release of the company's third quarter 2025 results. 
  • This purchase brings his total ownership to more than 575,000 shares. 
  • Amaze recently reported 44% sequential net revenue growth in the third quarter of 2025. Management expects to build on the momentum from its third quarter results. 
  • Amaze continues to expect to achieve near-profitability in the fourth quarter of 2025 and GAAP profitability in the first quarter of 2026, reflecting the seasonal strength in sales and operational improvements of the business.
  • Shares of Amaze Holdings (AMZE) were up more than 37% in premarket trading.

data.title: Newegg Commerce partners with PayPal for AI-powered shopping
data.description:
Newegg Commerce (NEGG) will integrate PayPal's (PYPL) agentic commerce services to enable AI-powered shopping on portals, including Perplexity.

Through the partnership with PayPal (PYPL), merchants like Newegg (NEGG) can become discoverable, and consumers can buy from merchants directly with Perplexity.

Newegg (NEGG) retains the merchant of record status to preserve customer relationships and brand visibility throughout the AI shopping experience.

“AI is redefining the customer shopping journey, and over the past two years we’ve leveraged AI to build entirely new experiences for our customers,” said Newegg CEO Anthony Chow.
